question.content: For the beam-rod coupled system, determine the rod force reaction B_y from superposition and compatibility at B. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression for the target quantity. Use the variable names from the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Use the force method: remove the rod constraint at B and take B_y as the single redundant reaction.
step_1.key_point: Primary structure is the simply supported beam under p_0.

step_3.content: From the primary beam, compute the free displacement Δ_B^0 at B, then apply a unit load at B to obtain flexibility f_BB.
step_3.key_point: This step uses the unit-load (virtual work) flexibility coefficient.

step_5.content: Treat the rod as an axial spring and write e=B_y/k with its axial stiffness k.
step_5.key_point: Rod deformation is not from beam bending; it is axial extension only.

step_7.content: Enforce compatibility at B: beam displacement reduced by the redundant force equals rod extension, i.e. Δ_B^0-B_y*f_BB=e.
step_7.key_point: Substitute e=B_y/k and solve one linear equation for B_y.
